desc:
You have reached a crossroads. To the west and east, the large corridor known as
Hwain's Way stretches his way seemingly forever; to the south, a tunnel leads up
inside a mining area. Finally, to the north, behind a slender arch carved in the
grey stone surrounding you, lies a small chamber. You can feel some strange
smell coming out of the chamber, but you cannot guess what might be its source.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
The long corridor that connects the West Door of Moria to the Deeps below East
Gate is crossing a minerary area. As usual, Hwain's Way proceeds in a west-east
direction, slowly descending as it approaches the eastern part of Moria. On both
sides of this huge corridor you can see many small passages leading up. Many of
the side tunnels have been closed off, but some still remain open.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
The old tunnel, which was once one of the most used connections between the City
of Moria and Moria's West Door, is approaching a wide shaft as it slowly
descends towards the Deeps. The air becomes cooler as one walks towards the
chimney, and the oppressive smell of Moria's denizens lessens, as if an air
current from the outside were carrying the heat and the stench away. Yet, the
relative absence of orkish smell is not reassuring, as the noise of clanking
boots can still be heard, sometimes faintly, sometimes more strongly.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
One of the most amazing architectural feats in Khazad-dum (and therefore in
Arda) suddenly reveals itself to all travelers who, having walked for a long
time on Hwain's Way, finally reach the Chimney of Durin. An extremely vast
chasm, running north, south, up and down as far as the eye can see, sunders the
mining part of Moria from the city's levels and deeps. Connecting the western
and eastern side of the abyss, a lattice of proud stone bridges almost fills the
empty space between the sheer-dropping cliffs. A slim stone tower stands in the
middle of the chasm, its foundations at the roots of Arda, its summit among the
peaks of the Misty Mountains; a spiral stair winds around it. Unfortunately, the
cataclismatic awakening of the Balrog severely damaged this place. Most of the
bridges, including all those leading to the central tower, have collapsed; most
of the tunnels ending in the Chimney have been blocked. This is not so, however,
for Hwain's Way, which continues to the east and west. A ledge also allows to
flank the western side of the abyss.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
This is the northern end of a ledge on the western side of an incredibly wide
and deep chasm. The ancient granite cliffs on both sides of the abyss are dotted
with tunnel exits, most of which seem to have been closed off. With a marvelous
feat of engineering, the Khazad built many arched stone bridges, connecting one
side of the chasm to the other. Currently, alas, most of the bridges have
collapsed, leaving only their truncated extremities as a monument to the
Dwarves' skill. A rare exception is the stout bridge east of here.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
You have reached the eastern side of Durin's Chimney, a wide and deep chasm
separating the mines of Khazad-dum from the halls of the old Dwarven city. With
an enormous effort, the Dwarven artisans once shaped the granite walls of this
place, building bridges to connect the tunnel endings on the two sides. A
slender, extremely elegant, cylindrical tower stands in the middle of the
Chimney, rising from unknown depths to heights far beyond the reach of your
eyesight. Stone steps, meant to give access to all of Moria's levels and deeps,
circle around the pillar. Unfortunately, as time passed, most of the bridges
crumbled and collapsed: there is no way to reach the tower from here, nor is it
possible to climb up and down to other levels. The only functional bridge in the
proximity leads west: it is part of Hwain's Way. Other bridges might be found by
following a narrow ledge to the north.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
You have entered what was once a vast complex of interconnecting minerary
tunnels. Now many of the galleries have collapsed, and there is no ore to be
seen anywhere. The only exits that have survived the ravages of time lead to a
large corridor to the south, and to a chamber to the west.
name: Exhausted Mines

desc:
The first thing you notice when entering this room is a huge, nearly vertical
shaft, placed roughly in the middle of the chamber. Some small recesses have
been chiselled in the walls of the pit, so that it is possible to climb it down.
A large stone block, which perhaps closed off the shaft, now lies broken into
pieces in a corner. Exits lead in all directions; the southern one is larger
than the others and crosses a corridor not far away, the northern one ascends
rapidly.
name: The Deep Shaft

desc:
You are in the middle of a maze of small minerary tunnels. Here the main tunnel
bends west and south; to the west the floor ascends steeply, while the southern
exit slopes gently downwards to a chamber. As you look at the walls, you realise
that the miners almost completed their work here before abandoning this place,
since there is nearly no trace of useful ore.
name: Exhausted Mines

desc:
You are at the edge of a vast complex of small and twisted minerary tunnels. The
Dwarves who dug them extracted almost all of the ore in this section of
Khazad-dum, before the Bane of Durin forced them to flee. The tunnels go on to
the west and the south, while to the north you can leave these narrow mines and
enter a large and straight corridor running from the west to the east.
name: Exhausted Mines

desc:
Here a narrow mine tunnel turns north, where it meets a much larger gallery, and
west, where it bifurcates into dozens of small passages. You can sometimes hear
the faint thumping of footsteps in the distance, and you wonder who, apart from
you, might be wandering along the tunnels of Moria.
name: Exhausted Mines

desc:
Small tunnels surround you in all directions, but most of them, as you explore
them, turn out to be dead ends - either because the Dwarves reached the end of
the ore vein, or because of a cave-in. The main tunnel leads north and west from
here, descending to the north and ascending to the west.
name: Exhausted Mines

desc:
You stand near the southwestern corner of a large, vaulted hall. The walls and
floor are of a polished stone blue-greenish in hue. The hall stretches away
north and east, receding into the gloomy murk. The western wall is dominated by
a grand avenue's entrance, framed by twin columns sculpted to resemble dwarven
lords.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
The large vaulted hall you stand in is dominated by a wide avenue entering from
the west. Stretching away on all other sides, the hall fades into the gloom. To
the east, at the centre of the room, lies the well that gives the name to this
ancient chamber.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
The northwestern corner of the hall is covered in a thin, dark dust, somewhat
unusual down here. Tracks of creatures large and small are faintly visible in
it, but give no clue to their makers any longer. Hwain's Well lies to the
southeast.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
The northern wall of this large hall is broken by a once grandly arched entrance
of an avenue. The pillars, now defaced, have lost their glory and stand ragged
and battered like rotting teeth. The hall stretches away into the gloom in all
other directions.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
You find yourself in the northwestern corner of a huge vaulted hall. The walls
are of a very strange blue-greenish colour, as is the floor. A steady current of
warm air, carrying the stench and the war cries of Morgoth's servants, blows in
your face from a dark avenue to the east.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
You are standing at the eastern entrance to the large vaulted chamber known as
Hwain's Well Hall. The Well, which is the reason for the hall's name, lies to
the west, in the middle of the room; the hall extends itself both to the north
and to the south, while to the east a corridor goes straight on to the extreme
reach of your sight.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
Bones, bones, bones everywhere. The pile of skulls, femurs and ribcages in this
corner of Hwain's Well Hall is truly enormous. Skeletons of all sizes, of
animals, of dwarves, of humans and even of trolls lie here, all massed together.
Evidently whoever lives here, after satisfying his huge hunger by eating
whatever and whomever was available, tossed here the remains. Hwain's Well Hall
goes on to the north and west.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
A roughly square vaulted hall, which the Dwarves dug from a lode of a strange
blue-greenish rock, lies north of you. In the centre of the chamber there is a
huge well, surrounded by a thick parapet. The smell of decay comes from a large
pile of bones in the southeastern corner of the room.
name: Hwain's Well Hall

desc:
A large well, enclosed by a thick wall of greenish-blue stone, attracts your
attention. The well lies at the exact centre of the large vaulted hall named
after the well itself. Thousands of minute runes and strange symbols have been
chiselled on the outer surface of the well; whoever defaced the rest of this
hall, somehow left the well alone - but for what reason, nobody knows.
name: Hwain's Well

desc:
A wide avenue with light grey walls begins here, in the Seventh Deep of Moria,
and solemnly strides to the east. Signs of the frequent passage of whoever
dwells here are abundant: the floor looks very worn, especially along the route
that connects the wide hall south of here to a chamber north. Other signs are
much more worrying: the loud clanking of metal boots, the disgusting smell
coming from the north, and the wanton destruction which was perpetrated on the
Dwarven artwork that once filled this corridor. One of the few beautiful things
still remaining in this place is a large carving on the western wall: it was
simply too large to destroy out of spite.
name: The Royal Way

desc:
The southeastern corner of an extremely dirty (and smelly) square room ends with
a wide archway on the southern side. The intense usage of the passage wore out
the hard stone of the floor, so that a small depression can now be found around
the portal. Sounds of clanking boots and occasional war cries can be heard all
around. The hall continues to the north, where the ground slopes down, and west,
where a small fire dissipates the gloom.
name: Filthy Hall

desc:
A pool of water, about ten paces diameter, has collected into this section of
the hall. Greenish-brown in colour, the water smells of well seasoned corpses. A
cursory glance is more than enough to justify the awful stench: gnawed bones,
some with pieces of rancid meat still attached, emerge from the surface near the
edges of the pool. No decent folk would even consider drinking this water;
unfortunately, the word "decent" can hardly be applied to the inhabitants of the
cave to the southwest.
name: Murky Pool

desc:
The northwestern corner of the hall, if possible even more messy and filthy than
the rest of the place, has been recently used as a sleeping area. A big pile of
straw, most probably brought here from the fields of Dimrill Dale, seems to have
been left here for ages; it has absorbed the typical disgusting smell of
Morgoth's corrupted perversions. Flickering rays of light from a small fire to
the south reach your eyes from time to time.
name: Filthy Hall

desc:
Around a small fire, used more to cook and to provide heat rather than to light
the room, a harsh yet pleasant scent of burned wood struggles against the
prevailing stench of decay tinging the entire hall. Whatever light the fire
provides, however, is more than enough to make the surrounding places darker by
comparison. The hall continues to the north and east; to the west, a tunnel with
a low ceiling begins its obscure path.
name: Filthy Hall

desc:
Gaping as the open mouth of some great, toothless beast, the eastern end of a
low and wide tunnel lies not far to the east. Faint light, coming from a small
fire in the hall beyond, creates deep, moving shadows on the walls, giving the
impression that, sooner or later, the tunnel will crush and slowly chew any
unfortunate person walking along it.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Each step taken along this wide and low gallery produces a very deep echo, which
reverberates through the ancient halls of the Seventh Deep. West and east the
corridor continues its journey, seemingly unchanged in the grey texture of its
smooth walls. Far to the east, a pinpoint of light might mark the end of the
road.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The ceiling is definitely low in this tunnel, as if the Dwarves had not bothered
to make it comfortable for taller people as well. What the road misses in
height, however, it makes up in width. Proceeding from the east to the west, the
colour of the surrounding stone seems to become darker, passing slowly from
light grey to dark grey and finally to pitch black.
name: Dark Road

desc:
In the middle of the thick darkness, the tunnel gently bends to the east and to
the southwest. The jet black, smooth stone all around seems to absorb any ray of
light, no matter how thin. Here and there, thin rivulets of dark water slowly
trickle down the walls, and collect into small cracks on the floor. The flow is
so slow and regular that it is nearly impossible to hear it; the oily smell of
the fluid, however, is overpowering.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The dark walls of this tunnel are slowly changing their texture. The cracks,
very common to the southwest, are becoming smaller and less numerous to the
northeast; in that direction, however, a thick smelly fluid slowly flows out of
some cracks and down along the walls, perhaps covering the smaller, barely
noticeable imperfections.
name: Dark Road

desc:
You have reached the southwestern end of a long, gloomy tunnel. Its dark grey
walls are full of cracks; a slow air current from the southwest raises, from
time to time, small puffs of dust from the floor. The usual smell of orcs, which
pervades the area all around, seems to have lessened; whether because the foul
spawn of Morgoth are actually far, or because of the air current, is not known.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Here the tunnel to the northeast opens into a wide road going southwards,
flanking the eastern side of an extremely vast chasm. Beyond a stone parapet,
which has collapsed in several places, looms the opposite face of the chasm, a
sheer cliff dotted with tunnel entrances. To the north, the south and above you
the abyss seems to continue endlessly, while, straining one's sight, one can
barely perceive a wide shelf perhaps a hundred feet below. A beautiful
cylindrical tower rises from the depths of Durin's Chimney and climbs into the
darkness above; a slim stair circles around the pillar. Sturdy bridges once
connected this side of the chasm to the tower and to the other side, but the
ravages of time made all of the bridges nearby collapse.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
You are now on the eastern edge of the huge chasm known as Durin's Chimney. A
majestic platform protrudes from the side of the precipice: built by the Dwarves
a long time before the coming of their bane, the platform allows any who stand
on it to gaze at both sides of the Chimney. Once an important crossroads in
Khazad-dum, this place is now usually deserted; the black uruks and the trolls
who now live here do not seem to appreciate the terrible beauty of this place.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
You have reached the western and lower end of a narrow stone bridge crossing the
Chimney. To the east the bridge climbs up towards the City of Moria, while north
and south a stone parapet surrounds the small rocky outcropping which supports
the end of the bridge. A strange-looking tunnel descends to the west.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
The glassy walls of the tunnel grow thicker and thicker as one follows them to
the west, until they finally merge in a smooth, hard, impenetrable transparent
wall which closes off the passage. One can only try to guess what happened in
these depths where the Dwarves dug for mithril, and what the Dwarf - whose
remains can still be seen, imprisoned in the translucent rock - saw before he
met a horrible, fiery death.
name: Glassy Tunnel

desc:
A long time ago a beautiful avenue used to start here, and to cross the entire
Seventh Deep of Khazad-dum from the west to the east. Now the road might still
run its ancient course, but it cannot be called beautiful any more. Chunks of
rock, the product of the Uruks' destructive hammering, lie at the feet of the
once finely chiselled marble pillars. What could have been a fine alabaster
statue is now nothing more than a pile of rubble.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
Your steps produce a faint, cavernous echo in the dark niches on both sides of
this corridor. Halfway between one niche and the next, the Dwarves of Khazad-dum
once erected mighty half-columns of white marble. The current inhabitants of
these halls evidently did not like the proud ornate pillars, since most of them
have been severely damaged - on purpose.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
A wide road, flanked by marble half-columns, runs from the west to the east. To
the west the avenue opens into a large vaulted chamber, whose walls show an
unusual blue-greenish tinge. From time to time, strange noises reach your ears -
seemingly from the western hall, though the echoes might be deceiving. To the
east Fror's Avenue stretches onward into the deep gloom.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
The elegant avenue known as Fror's Avenue stretches itself from the west to the
east. Hundreds of evenly spaced and finely engraved marble half-columns have
been built by the Dwarves along the walls. Something about the northern wall
attracts your attention: it seems that, once, some sort of tapestry hung from a
series of hooks near the ceiling.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
A three yard tall statue of an ancient Dwarf Lord dominates this section of the
corridor. Placed in a large niche on the southern side of Fror's Avenue, the
statue seems to be watching the surroundings. The Dwarf Lord is holding a large
hammer in his hand, while a set of jewelsmith's tools hang from his wide belt.
These signs of Khazad-dum's former wealth contrast deeply with the abandoned
halls in which you are walking.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
You are standing at the eastern end of one of the main corridors in the Seventh
and Last Deep of Khazad-dum. Large marble pillars flank the avenue's dark grey
walls as they stretch to the west, towards Hwain's Well. East of here Fror's
Avenue opens into a small, ornate chamber which seems to have been left
relatively untouched by the destructive fury of the Balrog's servants.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
About a dozen of large, lifelike statues of Dwarves meet the weary travellers as
they enter this vaulted, ornate chamber. The statues represent members of
Durin's People busy in the occupations they valued most: stonecraft, gem
cutting, metalworking. The emblems of the House of Durin have been engraved on
the eastern wall: the Crown, the Seven Stars, the Hammer and Anvil. To the west
lies a wide corridor known as Fror's Avenue; a chill, damp wind comes from a
dark road to the north. As you look around, you realise that this room has
suffered very little from the cruelty of the orcs of the Black Chasm. The
reason, however, lies beyond your grasp.
name: Ceremonial Hall

desc:
A wide, dark road bends gently from the northwest to the southeast. A faint
current of damp air comes from the north, brushes past you and loses itself in
some small crack in the tunnel floor. Every few paces a torch support hangs from
the walls; however, the torches have not been replaced in the last few
centuries. Now only the rusty supports remind the weary traveller who reaches
this forsaken place that, once, this was the heart of a prosperous kingdom.
name: Dark Road

desc:
A large, gloomy road extends itself from the north to the south. Originally a
natural cavern, this gallery was only marginally changed by the Khazad, who only
removed some stalactites and made the floor somewhat smoother. Due to this
choice, one might now be able to fully appreciate the sinister beauty of the
tunnel - were it not for the ever-present threat of marauding bands of orcs and
trolls.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The gaping tunnel to the south ends here, in a wide chamber almost completely
filled with dark, chilly water. Thin wisps of mist, similar to curled ghostly
fingers, pervade the air all around you. Strange shapes seem to be shifting in
the fog and below the surface of the lake: it takes you a while to realise that
it is most probably an illusion caused by the mist. Yet, the chance of some huge
and deadly monster stalking you under the cover of the fog is not to be
underestimated.
name: Shore of a Dark Lake

desc:
Dark water lies around you in all directions, covered by a thick coat of
shifting mist. The ceiling is getting dangerously near to the surface of the
water, except to the east, where the otherwise flat lake is disturbed by a
series of ripples, and to the south, where, through the mist, a small rocky
shore can be seen. The water is chilly; strange shapes can be seen below, but
whether they are mere reflexes of the fog or something else, none can say.
name: Dark Lake

desc:
Thick layers of mist, slowly rising from the surface of a nearly black lake,
surround you in all directions. The visibility is awful: one has to rely mostly
on the position of the low ceiling to see where he is going. To the east and
north, the free space above the water level quickly decreases to nothingness.
The otherwise calm surface of the lake is troubled, here, by a bubbling column
of air coming from the depths of the water.
name: Dark Lake

desc:
The dark water of the lake smoothly glides below you, as you cross one of the
thickest banks of fog you have ever met. The only way to understand where one is
going is to look the low, rough ceiling of this flooded chamber. In your
immediate surroundings, many huge stalactites protrude from the rock above you
and touch the water.
name: Dark Lake

desc:
The damp air current coming from the north whistles with a strange, almost
whimpering sound as it reaches this intersection. Droplets of water, which have
condensed on every surface around you, make the floor slightly slippery. Dark
roads leave to the south and to the north; looking ahead, the smooth walls of
the latter soon give way to the rougher stone of a natural cavern. Another
tunnel, extremely ornate, leaves west.
name: Dark Road

desc:
At its eastern end, the accessway to the Royal Chambers of Khazad-dum joins a
wide, dark road running south and north. As elsewhere in the City of Moria, this
corridor was enriched with fine art works; as elsewhere in Khazad-dum, the
devastating fury of the orcs has defaced the proud Dwarven masterpieces. On the
southern wall, among the foul scratches and coal marks, a relief has survived.
It seems to have been carved quite hastily, with little of the usual skill of
the Dwarves. Yet, when one stops for a moment and looks at the tragic scenes
represented on the wall, the reason for the haste becomes evident.
name: The Royal Way

desc:
A vast corridor, entrance to the King's Rooms, runs majestically from the west
to the east, with no intersections in sight. The Orcs' long hatred towards the
Dwarves can be clearly seen in the crude scratches which utterly ruined the
former beauty of this place. A large carving on the northern wall seems to have
attracted the "attentions" of the Uruks much more than anything else.
name: The Royal Way

desc:
A wide, ornate portal opens in the northern wall of a long corridor, stretching
itself west and east. The faint, metallic echo of heavy bootsteps reverberates
through the ancient halls of Khazad-dum, carried around by the massive rock. The
decorations in this part of the corridor have been left relatively untouched;
this allows any who pass to see the magnificent relief on the southern wall.
name: The Royal Way

desc:
You stand within a carefully-crafted chamber. The columns and arches are elegant
yet sturdy, while on the northern wall a relief depicts a Dwarf king in a
victorious pose. The shattered remnants of chairs and stone benches are found at
the walls. A large door, made from mahogany, fills a doorway to the west, while
empty archways lead east and south.
name: The Royal Antechamber

desc:
The ceiling of this passageway rises high above you; arched, it supports the
massive weight of the stone above it. As the corridor curves north and west, the
inner wall has Dwarven runes carven into it. The floor, even though made from
very hard stone, bears marks of wear.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
Although still curving gently, the corridor heads mostly north and south. A
large and solid door of oak stands in the middle of the eastern wall, and carven
into the wall, beside the door, are some runes. On the western wall the runic
writing continues its tale.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
The air about you is stagnant and dusty, filling the corridor with a stale
atmosphere. The high, arched ceiling is cracked in certain places, and the floor
has been chipped unevenly. The writing continues on the inner wall, and the
passage itself bends to the west and south.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
Although this room is cluttered with stored weapons, armour, and provisions,
behind the chests and shelves you can see the features of an abandoned armoury.
On the north wall, a great and cold forge lies dusty and filled with soot; on
the east, an anvil provides support for a table. A large, barred door is in the
south wall.
name: Converted Armoury

desc:
The curving corridor ends at a large door to the west. The runes on the wall
also end here, their message apparently complete. At the base of the northern
wall, the remains of a stone chest are scattered. Bits of twisted metal litter
the ground.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
Beneath the rubble, it is evident that this was once a stocked armoury. The
remainders of weapon racks and shelves and chests litter the floor, but now
everything has been taken, looted, or destroyed. The walls have been battered,
bearing large fist-marks. To the west, a door has been bashed from its hinges;
to the east, a door has survived the abuse with only moderate damage.
name: Royal Armoury

desc:
A large amount of rubble is on the ground. The eastern archway was hacked and
broken, and the door smashed. The walls themselves are chipped and marred, with
the writing on the south wall illegible. In the centre of the corridor, a small
aisle has been cleared away.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
The corridor continues to bend in a circle, and the runes still cover the inner
wall. A grand door is set in the outer wall, and to either side of it are stone
benches. The ground is well-worn here, scuffed by heavy feet. A cart partially
blocks the corridor south.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
This armoury has been maintained in a reasonable condition, no doubt for the use
of constructing weapons and armour. In fact, the forge is burning, and the air
is hot and sulphurous. Along the walls, niches carved into the stone hold tools
and material and half-finished items. A battered anvil stands in the northern
end of the chamber.
name: Royal Armoury

desc:
This grand chamber maintains a horrid magnificence. Its seven walls have been
blackened, and the ceiling has been covered in sable and purple tapestries.
Great columns rise from the floor and are enveloped into the tapestries. Most
striking is the throne in the centre of the room, made from stone and decorated
with gems and precious metals. On the dark floor are braziers, clouding the room
with a grey and incensed smoke.
name: The Great Throne

desc:
Northwards a great chamber stretches beyond you. The walls are black and dark
grey, and columns bear the weight of the mountain above you. The air is thick
with smoke, and before you is a large dish, filled with flame. It illuminates
the chamber with a harsh reddish light. East and west are double-doors, made of
a heavy and dark wood.
name: The Throne Room

desc:
Decrepit opulence fills this room. Cracked and discoloured tapestries from the
door to the east, and shattered busts stand to either side of it. An archway to
the south reveals a view of an avenue, which age and neglect have destroyed its
splendour. Westward a high-vaulted corridor starts to circle.
name: The Royal Antechamber

desc:
This passageway is circular, bending north and east. To the east, the passageway
leads into a once splendrous chamber. At the connection to that chamber, a
strange runic writing begins on the centre wall. On the ground, heavy footsteps
have smoothed the stone.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
As it goes north and south, this passageway gently curves eastward. Along the
eastern wall, runes trace out an intricate history. The stone floor has been
worn, and tracks of cart-wheels frame the scuffing done by boots.
name: Vaulted Corridor

desc:
The main access to the Royal Chambers of the House of Durin extends itself in a
west to east direction. In both directions wide, elegant portals open in the
northern wall not too far away. A weak stench reminding of rotten meat can be
felt to the west. To the north, between the portals, another part of the long
history of Khazad-dum was carved on the light grey wall.
name: The Royal Way

desc:
Advancing from the west to the east along this proud corridor, the disgusting
reek of orcs seems to lessen. Yet the signs of their presence nearby are more
than evident: only a totally corrupted follower of the Darkness would have
reduced the once magnificent halls of the Dwarves to their current pitiful
state. To the north, behind a portal, lies a small chamber, while to the south,
amid the wanton destruction, some of the original carvings have survived.
name: The Royal Way

desc:
This small antechamber connects to a large, heptangular chamber to the east. On
both the north and south walls, great frescos have been discoloured with age,
yet are still readable. Above you, in the ceiling, there sits a large crack,
centred on a thin hole. The smell of dust and decay is strong, and the stench of
trolls is conspicuously absent. To the west, a great oak door closes off the
doorway.
name: Entrance to the Royal Chambers

desc:
You stand amidst despoiled luxury. The plush, musty carpet beneath your feet
silences any footstep, no matter how harsh, and the vaulted ceiling's arches are
graced with crackling and discoloured purple sashes. All about you sit once
beautiful chairs, some broken and twisted by evil hands, some simply brittle
with age. In the southwest wall of this heptangular room a great table has been
set, its sturdy wood gashed deeply by fearsome claws. Still, some sanctity has
been preserved, and the taint of evil here is archaic. No forces, good or evil,
have reigned here for centuries.
name: King's Chamber

desc:
The bedrooms of the most powerful Dwarven rulers spread out, disused, around
you. A thick layer of dust coats the crumbling carpet, and it swirls chokingly
into the air as you disturb its rest. Cabinets and dressers and wardrobes line
the walls, with shattered mirrors besides them. Entrancing tessaracts have been
defaced, and priceless statues crushed. Grand beds, unslept in for centuries,
lie in the centres of the rooms.
name: Royal Bedrooms

desc:
This adjunct room next to the Lord of Khazad-dum's chambers served as the main
meeting room for him and his seven closest advisers. The well-hewn stone forms a
half-sphere shape, leaving no shadowed corners. An ancient octagonal table
dominates the centre of the room, its cured wood intact but showing slashes made
many centuries ago. The splinters of broken chairs lie strewn about the room,
covered in dust, evidence of destruction that took place well before. Not a
sound stirs in this ancient chamber, untouched for so long.
name: Conference Room

desc:
No comforts adorn this room. The floor, ceiling, and seven walls are perfectly
smooth stone, of varying shades of grey. A large statue stands before the
eastern wall, looking upon you with cold and distant eyes. This room is pervaded
by an awesome and strange feeling; the presence of Aule Mahal, the Builder,
resides in each stone. No claw marks or other marks of despoilment can be seen,
no walls are defaced, and the statue is not marred in any way. The dust that
lines the floor does not blanket the statue, and there is no smell of mould or
fungus.
name: Repose Room

desc:
A twin line of cracked marble pillars, leaning on the sides of this wide
corridor, continues to the west and to the east. Darkened niches open between
one column and the next. Looking inside the large holes in the avenue's walls
one can only see huge piles of rubble, but - perhaps - the next niche might hide
some nightmarish monster who would be delighted to add some more remains to the
rubble.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
A wide, elegant avenue stretches itself from the west to the east. Large marble
half-columns line the walls of the corridor; large niches open between one
column and the next. While the niches were originally crafted to house the most
beautiful works of art of Durin's people, now nearly all beauty has disappeared
from them.
name: Fror's Avenue

desc:
You have reached a small landing on an ancient, seldom used stair flanking the
Chimney of Durin. This is only a short respite for the person climbing the grey,
dusty steps, since the extremely steep ascent and descent resume after a few
meters. Narrow, jagged cracks can be seen on the walls, signs of the long time
elapsed since the construction of this stairway.
name: The Endless Stair

desc:
The damp mist enfolds you as you begin to plummet towards the distant earth. You
break through the bottom of the clouds and are rewarded with a brief but
spectacular view of the surrounding lands, but the onrushing ground quickly
squelches your sense of wonder.
name: Above the Clouds

desc:
A narrow, winding stone stair begins its twisted and secret path behind an arch
on the southern wall of this chamber, leading to some place far, far above. The
chamber itself is small, containing nothing but dust and a few empty torch
supports; its only exit, apart from the stair, is a plain stone door to the
west.
name: Bottom of a Hidden Stair

desc:
The old steps of a secret stone stair become gradually larger as they approach
the bottom; the twisted stairwell straightens as it rushes north to enter a
small room in the depths of Khazad-dum. A musty smell pervades the air around
you, hinting that this section of the City is usually closed off. Perhaps the
secret passages leading here have been forgotten by all; perhaps, on the other
hand, the final destination of this stair is a place so dreadful that the orcs
of the Black Chasm do not dare approach.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
More tortuous than the mind of a madman, the stair winds its way through the
hard rock of the Deeps of Moria. Though the horizontal direction keeps changing,
so that it would take a highly skilled scout not to lose one's bearings, the
slope of the stair remains always the same: the steepest that a Dwarf could ever
manage to climb without tools.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
Here the stair crosses a major fault line in the roots of the Misty Mountains.
During the passage of many centuries, the upper section of the stairwell has
slowly shifted down and sideways, partially blocking the way. The only passage
remaining is a small hole in a corner, barely large enough to squeeze through.
Dust and gravel from the cracked walls lie on the steps, making footing unsure
or worse.
name: Hidden Stair

desc:
Slightly bending counterclockwise, the Endless Stair continues its steep ascent
from the Deeps of Moria towards the upper levels of the old dwarven dwellings.
Climbing up is a strenuous task, since the sheer steepness of the stairway would
bring anyone to exhaustion in a short time. Climbing down is not easy either: a
false step would cause a ruinous - and probably deadly - fall.
name: The Endless Stair

desc:
An ancient stairway, carved in the stone of the mountain called Zirakzigil by
the Dwarves, Silvertine by the Humans and Celebdil by the Elves, begins here its
seemingly endless ascent. Its old, dusty steps are said to connect the deepest
dungeon of Khazad-dum to the Tower of Durin, on the peak of Celebdil. Considered
a legend for many and many years, the Endless Stair shows very few signs of
usage. Perhaps not even the orcs and trolls which now populate Moria have
discovered its existence.
name: The Foot of the Endless Stair

desc:
The corridor continues its course deep below the peaks of Caradhras, running
down from the east to the west. The floor shows the signs of intensive usage;
the ancient smooth walls, which were once clean, have been covered with a thick
layer of soot, which seems to absorb any, however tenuous, ray of light.
name: Dark Sloping Avenue

desc:
One of the many tunnels whick link together the wide halls of Moria reaches its
lower end a few paces to the west. The road is mildly sloping up to the east,
suggesting that the eastern end might belong to the Sixth Deep. To the west,
after a simple arch, the corridor opens into a large vaulted chamber with
greenish-blue walls.
name: Dark Sloping Avenue

desc:
Swimming about in this water-filled cavern you can see that this place was once
dry. A rough rocky wall and large stalactites indicate that the eroding feature
of the slow current has not had time to remove these features. Small cracks here
and there leak water out of this subterranean dead-end.
name: Underwater Cavern

desc:
Beyond the rocky crack, a small niche has been left undisturbed by the flow of
water. It looks like this has resulted in strange deposits encrusting the
chamber, as debris has found its way into here and then calcified. The only way
out is back into the cavern.
name: Underwater Niche

desc:
A narrow ledge, flanking the smooth, almost vertical, eastern wall of a
seemingly endless abyss, suddenly enlarges as it reaches its northern end.
Though the ledge was undoubtedly carved by the Dwarves to connect Hwain's Way to
another eastbound tunnel originating here, its purpose has not been fulfilled
for many and many years: either an earthquake or the destructiveness of Moria's
Goblins caused the eastern tunnel to collapse after a few meters. The bridge
connecting the collapsed tunnel to the other side of Durin's Chimney, on the
other hand, still stands in its place.
name: Durin's Chimney

desc:
The ancient Dwarven passage known as Hwain's Way ascends, as usual, from the
east to the west. The quantity of dust on the ground slowly increases as one
travels towards West Door; this might have something to do with the presence of
a very wide vertical chimney to the west. Eastwards the road proceeds in its
course; it should soon reach the chambers of the Deeps.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
You are walking along an ancient corridor in the depths of Khazad-dum. The grey
walls are full of small cracks, while the floor shows signs of heavy usage in
the past. From time to time a droplet of water falls from the ceiling and lands
with a faint 'plinck'.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
The old corridor known as Hwain's Way continues its slow descent towards what
was once the heart of Khazad-dum: the Royal Chambers of the Seventh Deep. From
the echoes you hear, coming from the east, you guess that you are not far from a
large chamber. Before the coming of the Balrog, the sounds of hundreds of busy
Dwarves would have met you here. This is not the case any more: now the faint
echo of heavy footsteps and of some occasional drum roll makes one wonder what
terrible, foul creatures are defacing the Dwarven halls.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
You have reached the eastern end of Hwain's Way, a large and straight corridor
connecting the Seventh Deep of Moria to the western entrance. To the east the
cracked grey walls acquire a blue-greenish tinge, as the corridor ends in an
enormous vaulted hall. To the west the road goes on and on, vanishing in the
darkness ahead; your feet ache as you think of how long it would take to walk
all the way from here to West Door.
name: Hwain's Way

desc:
A crack in the heart of the mountain widens here for some unknown reason. Above,
it quickly narrows and is impassable. Just below, an underground stream has
broken through the wall, filling it with water that drains into the unknown
depths below.
name: Air-filled Niche
